category_title: Timid students who feel voiceless and isolated in peer work
minimal_context: Brandon is standing in front of his 6th-grade science class to deliver a lesson as a co-teacher.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Brandon, please stand in front of the class and deliver the science lesson from the lesson plan.

STUDENT: (Stands in front of the class full of his peers) Okay...

TUTOR: Go ahead and teach them the science content.

STUDENT: (Talking slow) Today... we are... going to...

TUTOR: Keep trying.

STUDENT: (Very timid) I'm really nervous speaking in front of a class full of my peers. I'm talking slow... I usually feel like I have no voice because my partner doesn't like to talk to me.

options.A: Pre-planning specific responses to potential non-compliance when the student is asked to stand in front of the class and deliver the science lesson.
options.B: Introducing the science lesson by highlighting its importance for understanding daily issues, using scenarios, maintaining a rhythm for deep exploration, and providing feedback.
options.C: Establishing a norm where disagreement is acceptable and providing a platform for an evidence-based exchange of viewpoints during the science lesson.
options.D: Providing the student with the science lesson plan to review for homework in preparation to co-teach the class the following day.
